images.caption:
Figure 1:Overview of S3M-based Phonological Activation Mapping (SPAM).Left: Phonological vectors[14,15]can be found in S3M representation space by taking the difference of means. For example,𝐯voi+\mathbf{v}_{\texttt{voi+}}is the difference between the mean representations of voiced phones and other phones.Right:
S3M frame representations𝐫t\mathbf{r}_{t}and𝐫t′\mathbf{r}_{t^{\prime}}from different timestepsttandt′t^{\prime}are projected onto phonological vectors[15,38](e.g.,𝐯back+\mathbf{v}_{\texttt{back+}}), yielding activation values (top right). Stacking these projections from different phonological vectors for each frame produces a time-aligned activation mapping (bottom right).

Figure 3:Ablation on the training dataset size.
We randomly subsample TIMIT training split, which contains 4620 phonetically transcribed audio samples, from the full 3 hours training set (denoted11) down to1/10241/1024.
Figure 4:Segmentation performance on different S3Ms and layers for SPAM.
